title: The Palm and the Pine.
content:
From the German of Heine.



    In the far North stands a Pine-tree, lone,
    Upon a wintry height;
    It sleeps: around it snows have thrown
    A covering of white.

    It dreams forever of a Palm
    That, far i' the Morning-land,
    Stands silent in a most sad calm
    Midst of the burning sand.


    Point Lookout Prison, 1864.
meaning:
Sidney Lanier's contribution to classic is further solidified by the brilliance found in "The Palm and the Pine."...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
